title: Google DeepMind Research at Virginia Tech
headline: AI Research, accepted to ICLR, cited by Microsoft, Amazon, 30+ others
searchDescription: Frontier LLMs perform poorly on SealQA. Scalable RAG evaluation infrastructure with crawling, search-noise injection, retrieval, reranking.
highlightText: Researching with Dr. Tu Vu at the intersection of search and large language models. I am the second author on SealQA, accepted to ICLR. Our work focuses on scalable evaluation for search-engine-augmented LLMs, including data crawling, search-noise injection, retrieval, reranking, and multi-document reasoning. We also released the associated dataset on Hugging Face, where it reached about 20K downloads.

title: Google DeepMind Research at Virginia Tech
headline: π^2: Structure-Originated Reasoning Data Improves Long-Context Reasoning Ability of Large Language Models
searchDescription: Fully open-source realistic QA generation pipeline that SFT-ing on gpt-oss-20b gains an average of +4.3% absolute accuracy on 4 benchmarks: SealQA, LongBench-v2, Oolong-Synth, OfficeQA.
highlightTexts: We study a pipeline that curates reasoning data from initial structured data for improving long-context reasoning in LLMs.

We published a fully open-source realistic QA generation pipeline that SFT-ing on gpt-oss-20b gains an average of +4.3% absolute accuracy on 4 benchmarks: SealQA, LongBench-v2, Oolong-Synth, OfficeQA.

mediaFrames.caption: π^2 curation pipeline. We 1) collect tables from Wikipedia and expand them with new columns when the conditions are met, then for each table, 2) generate a multi-hop analytical reasoning question paired with an executable SQL query and verify the answer with an independent Python-implemented solution. Finally, we 3) produce structured analytical reasoning traces through back-translation.

title: Marriott International
headline: Software Engineer bringing product to life with AI
searchDescription: Built AI translation tooling, test systems, and release workflows for the Marriott Bonvoy app used by 237M+ members.
longDescription: At Marriott, I created an AI Translator system that reduced roughly 20 seconds per app translation by optimizing browser workflows with Azure OpenAI. I built and analyzed JUnit, regression, and automated end-to-end tests for the Marriott Bonvoy app, helping support a mobile product with 237M+ members. I also shipped Android v10.55 and iOS v10.87 using Eclipse, BrowserStack, QMetry, Harness, and Jira, and prototyped a real-time AI testing assistant that uses computer vision to detect screens, run iOS flows, and verify on-screen results.

mediaFrames.alt: Picture of me and CEO Anthony Capuano
mediaFrames.caption: picture of me and CEO Anthony Capuano

title: FPT Software
headline: Artificial Intelligence Engineer Intern for multimodal design systems
searchDescription: Built CV and NLP systems for architecture design generation and led AI art work for a UNESCO-partnered festival.
longDescription: At FPT Software, I built and integrated computer vision and NLP models that generated architecture designs from client requirements. I also led 5 interns to secure AI-generated art for a construction team, and that work was used in a UNESCO-partnered art festival. The role sharpened both my technical execution and my ability to coordinate creative AI work under real delivery pressure.

title: Lovealot.Together
headline: COO and Data Analyst for TikTok Channel
searchDescription: Built a TikTok ETL and analytics pipeline that processed 100K+ engagement signals across 10 countries and drove a 5% lift.
longDescription: As Chief Operating Officer and Data Analyst at Lovealot.Together, I built a TikTok ETL and analytics pipeline in Python and SQL that processed more than 100K engagement signals across 10 countries. That system improved content decision-making, helped drive a 5% engagement lift through automation, and supported partnerships with Vietnam’s #1 pay TV provider.

title: Virginia Tech
headline: Inspiring the next gen of talents as a Teaching Assistant
searchDescription: Supported CMDA 3654 and 1634 with Dr. Chris North, Dr. John Wenskovitch, and Dr. Eric Ufferman.
longDescription: Teaching Assistant for CMDA 3654 and CMDA 1634 at Virginia Tech. I support course delivery alongside Dr. Chris North, Dr. John Wenskovitch, and Dr. Eric Ufferman, helping students engage with quantitative, computational, and data-focused coursework in a more hands-on way.

title: Hybrid Electric Vehicle Team
headline: Data Engineer for 30-year-old Autonomous Vehicle Team
searchDescription: Synchronized 20 sensors for real-time object tracking of the Cadillac Lyriq using Python, SQL, and ROS 2.
longDescription: On the Hybrid Electric Vehicle Team, I work on data systems that support real-time object tracking for the Cadillac Lyriq. I synchronized 20 sensors using Python, SQL, and ROS 2, helping a multidisciplinary engineering team operate with production-like hardware and reliability constraints.
